     The Numbers Business Report

Neighbors 2: Sorority Rising (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $55,340,730Details
International Box Office $53,416,891
Worldwide Box Office $108,757,621
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,739,075
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,531,081
Total $6,270,156
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Now that Mac and Kelly Radner have a second baby on the way, they are ready to make the final move into adulthood: the suburbs. But just as they thought they’d reclaimed the neighborhood and were safe to sell, they learn that the new occupants next door are a sorority even more out of control than Teddy and his brothers ever dreamed of being. Tired of their school’s sexist, restrictive system, the unorthodox ladies of Kappa Nu have decided to start a house where they can do whatever the hell they want. When Shelby and her sisters, Beth and Nora, find the perfect place just off campus, they won’t let the fact that it’s located on a quiet street stand in their way of parties as epic as the guys throw.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$21,760,405 (39.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:50.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,384 opening theaters/3,416 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $76,517,353

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 20th, 2016 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: May 5th, 2016 (Wide) (Switzerland)
May 5th, 2016 (Wide), released as Bad Neighbours 2 (United Kingdom)
May 5th, 2016 (Wide) (Singapore)
May 6th, 2016 (Wide), released as Bad Neighbours 2 (Australia)
May 6th, 2016 (Wide) (Sweden)
Video Release: September 6th, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for crude sexual content including brief graphic nudity, language throughout, drug use and teen partying.
(Rating bulletin 2420 (Cert #50402), 4/13/2016)
Running Time: 92 minutes
Franchise: Neighbors
Plot point: Enemies Forced to Work Together, Neighbors from Hell, New Parents
Social setting: College, Fraternity/Sorority
Subgenre: Farcical / Slapstick Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Point Grey, Good Universe, Universal Pictures, Perfect World Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Now that Mac and Kelly Radner have a second baby on the way, they are ready to make the final move into adulthood: the suburbs. But just as they thought they’d reclaimed the neighborhood and were safe to sell, they learn that the new occupants next door are a sorority even more out of control than Teddy and his brothers ever dreamed of being. Tired of their school’s sexist, restrictive system, the unorthodox ladies of Kappa Nu have decided to start a house where they can do whatever the hell they want. When Shelby and her sisters, Beth and Nora, find the perfect place just off campus, they won’t let the fact that it’s located on a quiet street stand in their way of parties as epic as the guys throw.
